summary: First FPS foundation level is playable with camera-relative mobile movement and Pages cache-busting.
observations: The old Phaser MVP source, puzzle docs, Sokoban assets, and screenshots were removed.
Three.js is installed and the app boots into a mobile landscape debug arena.
The app exposes a debug snapshot API for future smoke and performance QA.
A 20 x 20 symbol-map level now drives placeholder walls, cover, endpoint marker, spawn, and collision.
Mobile touch controls support left-stick movement and right-side drag look.
decisions: Use docs/sub-agents as the sub-agent source of truth.
Require per-agent observations and handoff JSON.
Close spawned agents after final output is captured.
The current Three.js scene is a debug placeholder only, not final game art.
The symbol map is the level source of truth for this slice.
GitHub Pages deploy now includes no-Jekyll and SPA fallback files after build.
risks: A rewrite can sprawl unless each slice has an owner, gate, and stop condition.
External FPS assets have not been sourced, licensed, optimized, or tested yet.
Action buttons are visual placeholders until weapon and interaction systems are implemented.
